error_signature: Gmail trigger not firing after a day
title: Gmail trigger stops firing after a period
symptom: The Gmail trigger works initially but stops firing after a day, despite receiving emails.
root_cause: n8n Cloud uses polling to check for new emails, which can be rate-limited by Google. If the polling interval is too short, it may hit these limits, causing the trigger to stop firing after a while.
fix_steps: Open the Gmail Trigger node settings in your workflow.
Reduce the polling interval (e.g., change from 60s to 300s).
Ensure 'Mark as Read' or 'Remove Label' is enabled to prevent re-checking processed emails.
Check the Gmail credentials and click 'Reconnect' or 'Re-authenticate' to refresh the token.
agent_instruction: Adjust the polling interval and refresh the Gmail token in the node settings.
